micro_segments.title: Concept of Chalalah and Loss of Terumah Privileges

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Erusin vs. Nisuin: When Does a Woman Become Disqualified from Terumah (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Eliezer and Rebbi Shimon's Position: Only Nisuin Invalidates
Clarification: The Case Involves a Bat Kohen, Not a Yisraelit
Explanation of the Three Positions and Status Transitions

macro_segments.title: Gittin Status: Almanah or Gerushah After Nisuin vs. During Erusin

micro_segments.title: Full Invalidation After Nisuin: The Woman Becomes Permanent Chalalah
Reversal of Status if Husband Dies During Erusin Only

macro_segments.title: Baraita: Rebbi Meir's Kal Vachomer Argument and Its Refutation

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Meir's Kal Vachomer: If Kiddushei Reshut Exclude Her, Kiddushei Averah Certainly Should
Refutation: Marriage to Kohen (Even Forbidding Marriage) Keeps Her in Kohen Realm
Middle Zone: Not Pulled Away by Marriage to Kohen, Not Yet Chalalah

macro_segments.title: Case of Ptzua Daka Kohen Marrying Bat Kohen: Same Debate Applies

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Elazar Reporting Rebbi Oshaya: The Ptzua Daka Kohen Scenario
Distinction: Is the Problem Her Status or the Type of Kohen?
Yesh Lo Lehachil Bemakom Acher: The Kohen Must Be Able to Support Someone

macro_segments.title: Debate on Ptzua Daka Supporting a Wife: Abaye vs. Rava

micro_segments.title: Abaye: A Ptzua Daka Can Feed His Wife Without Having Had Sex
Rashi's Explanation: Marriage Occurred Before He Became Ptzua Daka
Proof from Continuity: If Early Status Dictated, Woman Should Keep Eating After Kohen Dies
Rava's Response: The Marriage Bond (Kinyan) Endures, Supporting Continued Terumah Status

macro_segments.title: Bat Gerim and Kohen Status: Rebbi Yochanan's Question to Rebbi Oshaya

micro_segments.title: The Unresolved Question: Can a Ptzua Daka Kohen Marry a Bat Gerim and Feed Her?
Rebbi Oshaya's Silence and the Later Question from Another Scholar
Rebbi Yehuda Nesia's Rebuke: Why No Answer for Rebbi Yochanan?

macro_segments.title: Analysis of Why There Is No Simple Answer: Rebbi Yehuda vs. Rebbi Yossi on Gerim Status

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Yehuda's Position: Kedushas Kehuna Restrictions Still Apply to Ptzua Daka
Rebbi Yehuda: Bat Ger Like Bat Chalal, Forbidden to Kohen and Ptzua Daka
Rebbi Yossi's Position: Bat Gerim Permitted to Both Kohen and Ptzua Daka
The Paradox: Differing Rules for Kohen vs. Ptzua Daka Restrictions

macro_segments.title: Rebbi Eliezer ben Yaakov: Distinguishing Bat Ger from Bat Giyores Based on Mother's Status

micro_segments.title: New Position: Bat Gerim Permitted to Kohen Only If Mother Is Yisraelit
The Unresolved Implication: Does Yisraelit Mother Affect Ptzua Daka Status?
Proof via Baraita: Ptzua Daka Kohen Can Feed Bat Gerim Terumah

macro_segments.title: Rebbi Eliezer ben Yaakov: Distinguishing Bat Ger from Bat Giyores Based on Mother's Status (continued)

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: Kashrah (Fitness for Kohen) vs. Kedushah (Holiness Toward Ptzua Daka)

macro_segments.title: Debate on Chuppah for Pesulot: Does Chuppah Without Consummation Invalidate a Woman?

micro_segments.title: Rav vs. Shmuel: Is Chuppah Meaningful for Pesulot (Forbidden Marriages)?
Rav's Position: Chuppah Not Meaningful for Underage Girls (Under 3 Years and 1 Day)
When Sex Is Possible: Girl Over 3 Years and 1 Day, Chuppah Acts as Invalidation
Enumeration of Consequences of Sex vs. Chuppah for Young Girls with Kohen
Conclusion: Chuppah Invalidates Only When Sex Is Halachically Possible

topical_tags.term: Chalalah (woman invalidated by forbidden sexual act with Kohen)
Rebbi Meir's position: forbids eating during Erusin with Kohen
Rebbi Eliezer and Rebbi Shimon: permit eating during Erusin with Kohen
Bat Kohen (daughter of Kohen) entitled to Terumah from father
Ptzua Daka (Kohen with crushed testicles - forbidden to marry)
Kehal Hashem vs. Kehal Gerim (naturally born Jews vs. converts' descendants)
Bat Ger (daughter of convert) and marriage restrictions
Bat Giyores (daughter of female convert) - Kohen marriage status
Rebbi Yehuda's position: Bat Ger forbidden to both Kohen and Ptzua Daka
Rebbi Yossi's position: Bat Ger permitted to both Kohen and Ptzua Daka
Rebbi Eliezer ben Yaakov: mother's status affects Kohen marriage eligibility
Kashrah (fitness for marriage to Kohen) vs. Kedushah (holiness status)
Three years and one day: threshold for sex to have halachic effect
Kinyan (acquisition/bond) in marriage affecting Terumah status
Kal Vachomer (logical inference): if true for permitted, more so for forbidden
